agents.name: Sarah Kim
agents.preferences_notes: One of the easier WME agents. Reads settlements carefully but fairly. Pet peeve: 'Miscellaneous' line items in expenses without itemization.

agents.name: Daniel Hwang
agents.preferences_notes: Pushes back hard. Wrote the email thread on the Coastal Spell dispute (March 2025). Tends to ambiguity in deal emails — worth pre-negotiating clarifications.

agents.name: Andrea Pelletier
agents.preferences_notes: Negotiates the deals; her colleagues handle settlement.

agents.name: Danny Ortiz
agents.preferences_notes: Easygoing. Trusts Mariana. Quick to sign off.

agents.name: Meera Patel
agents.preferences_notes: New at CAA, took over a roster from a departing agent. Still learning our venue.

agents.name: Pat Cho
agents.preferences_notes: Books smaller indie bands. Often the artist's manager too.

agents.name: Tom Neary
agents.preferences_notes: Has his own settlement template he wants filled in. Annoying but he renews the relationship.

shows.internal_notes: [Mariana, March 19] Settlement disputed by Daniel Hwang at WME re: marketing recoup interpretation. Marcus signed off on additional $720 to make it go away. See dispute-thread for full email chain. Going forward — get marketing recoup language explicit in the deal email.

deals.deal_notes_freetext: Flat $2,332. No upside.
$3,500 guarantee vs 85% of net after expenses, whichever greater. Renegotiated up from %-only deal three weeks before show — agent insisted on a floor. Expense cap $550, hospitality $300.
$1,405 guarantee vs 90% of net after expenses, whichever greater. Expenses capped $700. Hospitality cap $400. Performance bonuses per the deal memo (see email thread).
Flat $1,016. No upside.
Flat guarantee $2,516. Buyout deal.
Renegotiated 1 week before show: $3,931 g'tee vs 85/15 split on net (was 75/25). Expense cap $1950, hospitality $300.
Flat guarantee $1,373. Tour routing fill, no expenses.

deals.deal_notes_freetext: $2,631 vs 90% net + walkout pot. After breakeven on guarantee + expenses, all incremental gross goes to artist. Hospitality cap $400. +$400 if gross > $11,000; Walkout pot: 100% of gross above $3,200. [Updated 4 days before show via phone call with agent: bonus threshold dropped to $6,000. settlements.recoups_json: [{"id":"recoup_show_0001_late","category":"marketing","label":"Late-add: pre-show Instagram boost ($340)","amount":340,"status":"disputed"},{"id":"recoup_show_0001_misfiled","category":"production_overage","label":"Spotify pre-show ad spend recoup","amount":285,"status":"agreed"}]
settlements.notes: Paid out per the agreed line items. TM emailed two weeks later flagging the IG boost recoup — never resolved, never re-issued. Carrying as outstanding.

settlements.signoff_text: Looks good — TM. Wire to the usual account when ready.
settlements.notes: [Mariana, internal] TM signed off Sunday morning. His assistant emailed Monday questioning the production-overage line — that's why this is showing as disputed. Need to either re-send a clean version or close the dispute. Haven't gotten back to it.

settlements.signoff_text: OK — but flag any future marketing recoup deals.
settlements.notes: Disputed by WME (Daniel Hwang) on 3/18 over the $900 marketing recoup. Marcus authorized additional $720 to resolve, but the formal revision hasn't been pushed back into the system yet. Final agreed: $12,285 (vs originally calculated $11,565). See email thread for context. Going forward: deal emails must specify marketing recoup as inside or outside expense cap.
